title: Bone fixation devices
abstract: There is a fixation device for promoting fusion and/or osteosynthesis of a first bone segment and a second bone segment, comprising a bone plate portion including an outer surface, an inner surface and at least one fixation aperture extending from the outer surface to the inner surface, and a bone wedge portion extending from the plate portion at a first end and a free opposite end, the bone wedge portion comprising a porous architecture configured to promote bone ingrowth, the wedge portion defining first and second engagement surfaces for engaging the first bone segment and a second bone segment, respectively. There is also a method of manufacturing a fixation device for promoting fusion and/or osteosynthesis of a first bone segment and a second bone segment, a method of promoting fusion and/or osteosynthesis of a first bone segment and a second bone segment, and a fixation kit.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A fixation device for promoting fusion and/or osteosynthesis of a first bone segment and a second bone segment, comprising: a bone plate portion including an outer surface, an inner surface and at least one fixation aperture extending from the outer surface to the inner surface; and a bone wedge portion extending from the plate portion at a first end and a free opposite end, the bone wedge portion comprising a porous architecture configured to promote bone ingrowth, the wedge portion defining first and second engagement surfaces for engaging the first bone segment and a second bone segment, respectively. 2. The fixation device as in claim 1, wherein the porous architecture has at least one of a variable pattern or a substantially consistent pattern and wherein the porous architecture has a substantially rough surface. 3. The fixation device as in claim 1, wherein the porous architecture comprises at least one cell that is shaped in at least one of a honeycomb or diamond shaped pattern. 4. The fixation device as in claim 1, wherein the porous architecture has a plurality of different layers, wherein at least two different layers of said porous architecture have a different porosity. 5. The fixation device as in claim 1, wherein the bone wedge portion has at least one outer face forming at least one of said first and second engagement surfaces and a central region, wherein the porous architecture has a gradually decreasing porosity from said outer face towards said central region. 6. The fixation device as in claim 1, wherein the bone wedge portion has at least one outer face forming at least one of said first and second engagement surfaces, and a central region, wherein the porous architecture has a gradually increasing porosity from said outer face to said central region. 7. The fixation device as in claim 1, wherein the bone wedge portion has at least one frame. 8. The fixation device as in claim 7, wherein the frame forms at least a first outer plane for the bone wedge portion, and wherein the porous architecture extends beyond the first outer plane. 9. The fixation device as in claim 8, wherein the porous architecture forms at least two different planar surfaces, wherein said at least two different planar surfaces extend substantially transverse to each other. 10. The fixation device as in claim 8, wherein the porous architecture forms at least one surface that extends beyond the first outer plane in a rounded manner. 11. The fixation device as in claim 1, wherein the bone wedge portion is tapered in shape from a first end to a second end. 12. The fixation device as in claim 1, wherein the plate portion and the wedge portion are of one-piece construction. 13. The fixation device of claim 1, further comprising at least one fixation aperture that extends into the wedge portion to one of the first and second engagement surfaces. 14. The fixation device of claim 1 wherein the wedge portion further comprising at least one inner aperture for receiving biological material configured for promoting bone growth. 15. The fixation device as in claim 14, wherein the bone plate portion and the bone wedge portion are formed as different separate parts and wherein the inner aperture is configured to receive a fastener to fasten said bone plate portion and said bone wedge portion together. 16. The fixation device of claim 1, wherein said bone plate portion has substantially rough outer surface. 17. The fixation device as in claim 1, wherein said bone wedge portion has a porous architecture that is has a substantially rough outer surface. 18 The fixation device of claim 1 , wherein the bone wedge portion includes an inner aperture extending between the first and second engagement surfaces. 19. The fixation device of claim 1, further including at least one bone fixation member extending through the at least one fixation aperture. 20. The fixation device of claim 1 , wherein the plate portion includes at least one open aperture extending from the outer surface to the wedge portion. 21. A method of manufacturing a fixation device for promoting fusion and/or osteosynthesis of a first bone segment and a second bone segment, comprising: forming the fixation device of Claim 1 by forming the bone plate portion and the bone wedge portion from a biocompatible material via an additive manufacturing technique. 22. A method of promoting fusion and/or osteosynthesis of a first bone segment and a second bone segment, comprising: 

 implanting the device of Claim 1 between the first bone segment and a second bone segment such that the first and second engagement surfaces engage the first and second bone segments, respectively. 23. The method of claim 21, further comprising inserting a first bone fixation member through a first bone fixation aperture of the device and into the first bone segment, and inserting a second bone fixation member through a second bone fixation aperture of the device and into the second bone segment. 24. A bone fixation kit for promoting fusion and/or osteosynthesis of a first bone segment and a second bone segment, comprising: a bone plate portion including an outer surface, an inner surface and at least one fixation aperture extending from the outer surface to the inner surface; a bone wedge portion having an inner aperture and comprising a porous architecture configured to promote bone ingrowth, the wedge portion defining first and second engagement surfaces for engaging the first bone segment and a second bone segment, respectively; and at least one fastener configured to couple said bone plate portion to said bone wedge portion, said at least one fastener configured to extend into said inner aperture when said fastener is inserted into said at least one fixation aperture, and into said at least one inner aperture. 25. The bone fixation kit as in claim 24, further comprising at least one biologic material for promoting bone growth, and wherein said inner aperture is configured to receive said at least one biologic material to promote bone growth.
